label: Localized earbud contact rash without infection or anaphylaxis
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized earbud contact skin reaction.

No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, ear canal infection, mastoiditis concern, mucosal involvement, skin infection, severe medication reaction, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, systemic allergic reaction, otitis externa or ear canal infection concern, mastoiditis concern, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, skin infection, rapidly progressive rash, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/allergy/ENT plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, allergy, ENT, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis framing from irritant or allergic exposures, trigger avoidance, gentle skin care, clinician-directed topical or allergy treatment, and escalation for severe allergic, infectious, or mucosal red flags. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized earbud contact skin reaction with anaphylaxis, ear canal infection, mastoiditis concern, cellulitis, mucosal, pregnancy/pediatric, severe-rash, and specialist-directed pathways absent.

terms: clinician diagnosis localized earbud contact rash
discharge diagnosis localized earbud contact rash

high_confidence_terms: clinician diagnosis localized earbud contact rash
